last_digest.title: Xsolis Data Breach Impacts 1.4 Million People
last_digest.rss_summary: Xsolis disclosed a breach affecting 1.4M people after a phishing attack exposed personal and health data from its hospital clients’ systems. Healthcare tech company Xsolis, Inc. has disclosed a data breach impacting nearly 1.4 million individuals. The Tennessee-based firm provides utilization management and revenue cycle solutions for healthcare providers. The company became aware of an […]

last_digest.title: LastPass confirms data breach in Klue supply chain attack
last_digest.rss_summary: LastPass announced that hackers accessed customer data from its Salesforce environment after stealing the company's OAuth tokens in the Klue supply chain attack earlier this month. [...]

last_digest.title: FortiBleed Attackers Turn Firewalls Into Credential Stealers as Heists Persist
last_digest.rss_summary: The threat actors engineered a Golang-based sniffer to target 430,000 FortiGate firewalls and identify 110 million credentials in the ongoing global campaign.

last_digest.title: The Global Namespace Risk: Universal Bucket Hijacking Technique for Cloud Data Exfiltration
last_digest.rss_summary:
Unit 42 research details how attackers could exploit global name uniqueness in bucket hijacking to redirect cloud data streams across major CSPs.
The post The Global Namespace Risk: Universal Bucket Hijacking Technique for Cloud Data Exfiltration appeared first on Unit 42.

last_digest.title: Data Exposure Flaws Threaten Dify AI Platform Used by 1 Million Apps
last_digest.rss_summary:
Attackers could abuse Dify's multi-tenant cloud service to read private chats, preview other tenants' documents, and reach internal APIs.
The post Data Exposure Flaws Threaten Dify AI Platform Used by 1 Million Apps appeared first on SecurityWeek.

last_week_digest.title: In Other News: Google Security Layoffs, AudiA6 Takedown, $400 Million Coupang Fine
last_week_digest.tldr: South Korean retail giant Coupang faces a record $400 million fine for manipulating search rankings, highlighting increasing regulatory scrutiny over algorithmic business practices. Meanwhile, global security teams face heightened operational risks as Google implements workforce reductions and major corporations like IBM and AT&T confront allegations of covering up significant data breaches, signalling a volatile period for industry accountability.

last_week_digest.title: Iran-Linked Handala Breached a California Water Utility. It Could Have Done Worse, and It Knows That.
last_week_digest.tldr: The Iran-linked group Handala breached California Water Service, exfiltrating 5GB of data including billing records for two million customers. By exploiting an exposed GPS tool to pivot into internal networks, the attackers demonstrated significant operational reach. This incident highlights critical vulnerabilities in utility infrastructure, signalling that inadequate network segmentation poses severe risks to global essential services.

last_week_digest.title: Oracle PeopleSoft RCE Flaw Used as Zero-Day in Ongoing ShinyHunters Campaign
last_week_digest.tldr: The threat group ShinyHunters exploited a critical zero-day vulnerability in Oracle PeopleSoft, compromising over 100 organisations, primarily universities, between May 27 and June 9. This campaign highlights the severe operational risks posed by unpatched enterprise software, signalling that attackers are increasingly weaponising remote code execution flaws to gain persistent access and facilitate widespread data extortion.

last_week_digest.title: Early Warning Signs of Supply-Chain Attacks Live in the Dark Web
last_week_digest.tldr: Research by Flare reveals that early indicators of software supply-chain attacks, such as compromised GitHub credentials and OAuth tokens, frequently appear on dark web forums before public disclosure. These breaches threaten global business operations by exploiting trusted third-party integrations. This trend signals that organisations must proactively monitor underground marketplaces to mitigate escalating systemic risks.

last_week_digest.title: Novo Nordisk reports cyberattack as UK gives Wegovy pill the nod
last_week_digest.tldr: Pharmaceutical giant Novo Nordisk recently suffered a cyberattack involving the theft of pseudonymised clinical trial data and healthcare partner contact details. While core operations remain unaffected, the breach exposes participants and partners to targeted phishing risks. This incident signals that global life sciences firms face escalating threats, necessitating heightened vigilance regarding sensitive research data.

last_week_digest.title: Kodak confirms data breach claimed by ShinyHunters extortion gang
last_week_digest.tldr: Kodak recently confirmed a security breach after the ShinyHunters extortion gang claimed to have stolen over 2.2 million records containing sensitive customer and corporate data. This incident highlights the persistent threat posed by organised cybercriminal groups, signalling that even established global enterprises remain highly vulnerable to large-scale data exfiltration attacks.

last_week_digest.title: China-linked actor spent two years inside medical research networks
last_week_digest.tldr: The China-linked group UNC6508 infiltrated North American medical and military research networks for two years, exfiltrating sensitive data via compromised REDCap servers. This espionage campaign highlights critical visibility gaps in legacy infrastructure, signalling a shift towards long-term, stealthy intellectual property theft that threatens global clinical research and public health security.

last_week_digest.title: 'Lorem Ipsum' Malware Pivots to ClickFix Delivery
last_week_digest.tldr: Following Microsoft’s revocation of over 1,000 fraudulent certificates, the Lorem Ipsum malware group shifted from Trojanised installers to ClickFix lures on compromised WordPress sites. This evolution broadens the victim pool across global sectors, signalling that sophisticated threat actors are rapidly adapting their delivery models to bypass enhanced digital security infrastructure.

last_week_digest.title: iRhythm Confirms Data Stolen in Hack
last_week_digest.tldr: Digital health provider iRhythm recently confirmed a significant data breach discovered on 8 June, where attackers successfully exfiltrated sensitive information and issued ransom demands. This incident disrupts operational continuity and highlights the growing vulnerability of healthcare infrastructure, signalling an urgent need for global organisations to bolster defences against extortion-based cyberattacks.

last_week_digest.title: India temporarily blocks Telegram over medical exam cheating fears
last_week_digest.tldr: Indian authorities temporarily blocked Telegram and restricted its editing features to prevent cheating during a major medical entrance exam affecting two million candidates. This move, following fraud ring arrests involving 15 million rupees, signals a growing trend of governments disrupting digital platforms to maintain institutional integrity despite significant operational collateral.

last_week_digest.title: EdTech Faces a Cybersecurity Crisis: Data Breaches Surge
last_week_digest.tldr: Cybercriminals like ShinyHunters and FulcrumSec are increasingly targeting EdTech, recently compromising 137,000 staff accounts and disrupting global school operations. These breaches highlight systemic vulnerabilities in educational infrastructure, signalling an urgent need for enhanced security protocols as the sector becomes a primary target for extortion.

last_week_digest.title: FulcrumSec Targets Novo Nordisk, Leaks Clinical and Research Data
last_week_digest.tldr: The extortion group FulcrumSec leaked 1.3TB of data from Novo Nordisk after a failed $25 million ransom demand. The breach exposed clinical trial records and proprietary AI research assets, signalling a shift where cybercriminals now target critical intellectual property to cripple pharmaceutical competitive advantages.

last_week_digest.title: EU grants Ukraine access to cybersecurity reserve for major attacks
last_week_digest.tldr: The European Union has granted Ukraine access to its cybersecurity reserve, enabling rapid deployment of private experts during major incidents. This integration signals a shift toward collective digital defence, bolstering cross-border resilience and deepening strategic security cooperation between Kyiv and the European bloc.

last_week_digest.title: Attackers hit pair of critical Fortinet vulnerabilities the vendor disclosed in April
last_week_digest.tldr: Attackers are actively exploiting critical FortiSandbox vulnerabilities, with 49 incidents recorded across nine countries. By compromising these trusted security appliances, threat actors gain elevated network access. This trend signals that enterprise security tools are increasingly becoming primary targets for global, multi-operator exploitation campaigns.

last_week_digest.title: Kodak Admits Data Breach After ShinyHunters Hack Claims
last_week_digest.tldr: Kodak has confirmed a data breach following claims by the hacking group ShinyHunters, though the company asserts that its core systems and operations remain unaffected. This incident highlights the persistent threat posed by extortionist actors targeting global corporate entities to compromise sensitive information.

last_week_digest.title: GentleKiller targets more than 400 security processes across 48 products
last_week_digest.tldr: The ransomware gang Gentlemen has developed GentleKiller, a sophisticated toolset capable of disabling over 400 security processes across 48 products. By centrally managing these EDR-killing capabilities for affiliates, the group has become a top-five global threat. This shift signals a professionalisation of ransomware operations, prioritising rapid evasion to ensure successful, large-scale data extortion.

last_week_digest.title: Klue OAuth breach linked to 'Icarus' Salesforce data theft attacks
last_week_digest.tldr: The Icarus threat group exploited Klue’s OAuth integration to exfiltrate sensitive Salesforce data from multiple global organisations, triggering widespread extortion attempts. Salesforce subsequently disabled the affected application to mitigate further risk. This breach underscores the critical operational vulnerabilities inherent in third-party integrations, signalling a growing industry trend of targeting SaaS supply chains for data.

last_week_digest.title: Malware attacks strip Roblox developers of entire games
last_week_digest.tldr: Malware campaigns are increasingly targeting game developers, with attackers hijacking Roblox titles and stealing virtual currency through sophisticated social engineering. These breaches, which disrupt primary income streams for creators, highlight critical vulnerabilities in platform trust models. Such incidents signal a growing trend where cybercriminals exploit collaborative ecosystems to compromise professional accounts and assets globally.

last_week_digest.title: Dream Raises $260 Million at $3 Billion Valuation
last_week_digest.tldr: Israeli startup Dream has secured $260 million in fresh funding, achieving a $3 billion valuation to expand its sovereign AI and cyber defence capabilities for governments and critical infrastructure. This significant capital injection underscores a growing global industry shift towards prioritising national security and protecting essential digital assets against increasingly sophisticated state-sponsored cyber threats worldwide.

last_week_digest.title: Australian sugar producer works to restore operations as ransomware group claims attack
last_week_digest.tldr: The ransomware group Gentlemen has compromised Mackay Sugar, Australia’s second-largest producer, forcing two mills to suspend operations during the critical crushing season. This breach, impacting a firm with $420 million in annual revenue, highlights the escalating threat of double-extortion tactics against essential supply chains, signalling that industrial infrastructure remains a primary target globally.

last_week_digest.title: Klue OAuth breach victim list grows as Icarus hackers claim attack
last_week_digest.tldr: Market intelligence firm Klue suffered a breach after hackers exploited legacy credentials to steal OAuth tokens, enabling unauthorised access to Salesforce environments at major organisations including Jamf and Tanium. This incident highlights critical supply chain vulnerabilities, signalling that third-party integrations represent a significant risk vector for data exfiltration and targeted follow-on social engineering campaigns.

last_week_digest.title: SECURITY AFFAIRS MALWARE NEWSLETTER ROUND 102
last_week_digest.tldr: A recent supply chain attack against OptinMonster compromised 1.2 million websites, highlighting the severe operational risks posed by third-party software vulnerabilities. This incident, alongside sophisticated campaigns targeting medical research and developer tools, signals a dangerous industry trend where attackers increasingly weaponise trusted infrastructure to bypass traditional defences, necessitating more rigorous supply chain security oversight globally.

last_week_digest.title: Inside GentleKiller: The EDR-Killer Powering The Gentlemen
last_week_digest.tldr: The ransomware syndicate known as The Gentlemen has compromised 504 victims, emerging as a top-tier global threat by centralising EDR-killer tools for affiliates. By weaponising vulnerable drivers, this group lowers entry barriers for attackers. This shift signals a dangerous industry trend where operators prioritise standardised, rapid-deployment toolkits to systematically neutralise enterprise security defences globally.

last_week_digest.title: Police raid malware network tied to Russia's Evil Corp hacker group
last_week_digest.tldr: International law enforcement dismantled the SocGholish botnet, seizing over 100 servers and cleaning 15,000 compromised websites linked to the notorious Evil Corp group. This operation disrupts a critical entry point for global ransomware campaigns, signalling that coordinated cross-border police action is increasingly targeting the infrastructure supporting widespread financial extortion and persistent cyber espionage threats.

last_week_digest.title: Cybersecurity Firms Impacted by Klue Supply Chain Attack
last_week_digest.tldr: Klue recently suffered a supply chain attack involving the exfiltration of sensitive data from Salesforce instances belonging to prominent cybersecurity firms, including Huntress and Recorded Future. This breach highlights critical vulnerabilities within interconnected SaaS ecosystems, signalling that third-party vendors now represent a significant systemic risk, forcing global enterprises to urgently re-evaluate their supply chain security posture.

last_week_digest.title: WhatsApp phishing attack uses fake business docs to hack PCs
last_week_digest.tldr: A global WhatsApp phishing campaign is compromising business accounts to distribute malicious VBScript files, granting attackers remote administrative access. Spanning twelve countries, this sophisticated operation signals a growing trend of leveraging trusted communication channels to bypass security, forcing organisations to re-evaluate internal verification protocols.

last_week_digest.title: ShapedPlugin Supply Chain Attack Backdoors Pro Plugin Updates
last_week_digest.tldr: Attackers compromised ShapedPlugin’s build pipeline, injecting backdoors into three Pro WordPress plugins. This supply chain attack, affecting hundreds of thousands of sites, exfiltrated credentials and 2FA secrets. It signals a growing industry trend where trusted software update channels are weaponised to bypass standard security defences.

last_week_digest.title: Phishing hides in routine Microsoft 365 workflows
last_week_digest.tldr: Attackers are exploiting Microsoft 365 collaboration features, including Outlook Groups and calendar invites, to bypass traditional security filters. By embedding phishing lures within trusted productivity workflows, threat actors increase credential theft risks, signalling that organisations must now treat internal collaborative tools as high-risk attack vectors.

last_week_digest.title: What the Latest ShinyHunters Breaches Reveal About Modern Cyberattacks
last_week_digest.tldr: ShinyHunters’ recent breaches, exposing millions of user records, demonstrate that attackers increasingly bypass complex malware to exploit systemic vulnerabilities. These incidents disrupt global operations and erode consumer trust, signalling a critical industry shift towards prioritising robust identity management over traditional perimeter-based security defences.

last_week_digest.title: Canadian utility fesses up to data breach, but key details remain off-grid
last_week_digest.tldr: London Hydro is investigating a security breach potentially exposing the personal data of over 160,000 customers. While financial details remain secure, the incident highlights how utility providers face increasing risks, signalling that critical infrastructure firms must prioritise data transparency to maintain public trust.

last_week_digest.title: Xsolis Data Breach Impacts 1.4 Million People
last_week_digest.tldr: Healthcare technology firm Xsolis recently suffered a data breach after a phishing attack exposed the sensitive personal and medical information of 1.4 million individuals. This incident disrupts client operations and highlights the critical vulnerability of third-party service providers, signalling an urgent need for enhanced supply chain security across global industries.

last_week_digest.title: LastPass confirms data breach in Klue supply chain attack
last_week_digest.tldr: LastPass suffered a data breach after the Icarus group exploited a Klue supply chain vulnerability to steal OAuth tokens. Attackers accessed Salesforce CRM data, exposing customer contact and support information. This incident highlights the critical operational risks posed by third-party integrations, signalling a growing industry need for rigorous vendor security.

last_week_digest.title: FortiBleed Attackers Turn Firewalls Into Credential Stealers as Heists Persist
last_week_digest.tldr: The FortiBleed campaign has compromised 430,000 FortiGate firewalls globally, enabling attackers to harvest 110 million credentials using a custom sniffer tool. This breach of high-value defense contractors signals a shift toward weaponising perimeter infrastructure, forcing organisations to recognise that compromised network hardware now serves as a persistent, scalable threat vector.

last_week_digest.title: The Global Namespace Risk: Universal Bucket Hijacking Technique for Cloud Data Exfiltration
last_week_digest.tldr: Researchers have identified a critical architectural flaw affecting major cloud providers, where attackers can hijack globally unique storage buckets to reroute sensitive data streams. This vulnerability poses significant operational risks, potentially enabling silent exfiltration of logs. It signals a shift toward prioritizing immutable identity verification over predictable, name-based cloud infrastructure configurations.

last_week_digest.title: Data Exposure Flaws Threaten Dify AI Platform Used by 1 Million Apps
last_week_digest.tldr: Critical vulnerabilities in the Dify AI platform have exposed sensitive data across one million applications, allowing unauthorised access to private chats, internal APIs, and tenant documents. This incident underscores the systemic risks inherent in multi-tenant AI infrastructure, highlighting how platform-level flaws can compromise global business operations and undermine enterprise data security.
